message:
chore(beads): file 3 bugs surfaced by fresh-eyes review

- franken_networkx-9x7r0 (P1): MultiGraph.copy() (and three sibling
  view-class copies) crash when an edge attribute is literally named
  'key' — the implementation uses ``add_edge(u, v, key=key, **attrs)``
  which collides when attrs contains a 'key' entry. Applies to
  ec93156, 7cafa35, and three pre-existing view-class copies.
- franken_networkx-7symx (P2): fnx.union(G, H, rename=...) returns
  an nx graph instead of an fnx graph.
- franken_networkx-3ehfp (P2): Broader scope of 7symx — bfs_tree,
  random_tournament also return nx graphs. Subsumes 7symx.

All three found by reviewing 20+ commits of the recent drain work;
each has a minimal reproducer and a fix sketch in the description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Likely an algorithmic complexity issue with node connectivity on the test graph.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:43:32.900355089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:47:10.120344702Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T13:47:10.119637784Z","close_reason":"Fixed infinite loop in local_node_connectivity_bfs: when BFS found a direct s->t edge (path length 1), nothing was excluded, causing the same path to be found forever. Fix tracks used direct edges to prevent re-discovery.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-3e5","title":"Expose flow algorithm completeness to Python (min_cost_flow, gomory_hu, disjoint paths)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:16:04.536561723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:31:51.381165816Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:31:51.380902147Z","close_reason":"Already completed: gomory_hu_tree, edge_disjoint_paths, node_disjoint_paths wired to Rust in bead h1o","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-3ehfp","title":"Delegated public exports return nx graphs instead of fnx graphs (bfs_tree, random_tournament, union)","description":"Discovered during fresh-eyes review of 057a442 (delegation refactor)
and predecessors. Three public exports delegate their core work to
upstream networkx and then return the nx graph result **without
round-tripping it back to the fnx Python surface**:

1. ``fnx.bfs_tree(G, source, ...)`` → returns nx.DiGraph
   (python/franken_networkx/__init__.py::bfs_tree, routes through
   ``_call_networkx_for_parity("bfs_tree", ...)`` in
   ``_call_networkx_for_parity`` at line ~1523).

2. ``fnx.random_tournament(n, seed=...)`` → returns nx.DiGraph
   (``_random_tournament_via_parity``).

3. ``fnx.union(G, H, rename=...)`` with non-empty ``rename`` → returns
   nx.Graph/DiGraph of whatever matches G/H
   (``_union_with_rename_via_parity``). This subsumes the originally
   filed bead ``franken_networkx-7symx``.

Reproducer::

    import franken_networkx as fnx

    g = fnx.Graph(); g.add_edges_from([(0,1),(1,2),(2,3)])
    assert isinstance(fnx.bfs_tree(g, 0), fnx.DiGraph)            # FAILS
    assert isinstance(fnx.random_tournament(5, seed=42), fnx.DiGraph)  # FAILS
    assert isinstance(fnx.union(g, g, rename=('a-','b-')), fnx.Graph)  # FAILS

Users calling an fnx function expect fnx types — subsequent native
dispatch (Rust-backed algorithms, backend caching, PlanarEmbedding
handoff, pickling) assumes the fnx surface. Silently returning nx
objects causes surprise failures like "TypeError: expected Graph,
DiGraph, MultiGraph, or MultiDiGraph" deep in later call chains.

The already-working reference pattern is ``_random_kernel_graph_via_parity``
which wraps with ``_from_nx_graph(g)`` before returning.

Fix template for each helper::

    from franken_networkx.readwrite import _from_nx_graph

    def _bfs_tree_via_parity(G, source, ...):
        import networkx as _nx_mod
        nx_result = _nx_mod.bfs_tree(_networkx_graph_for_parity(G), source, ...)
        return _from_nx_graph(nx_result)

For ``_call_networkx_for_parity`` (the generic submodule helper),
either:
(a) add an opt-in ``convert_result=True`` flag for graph-returning
    callers, or
(b) detect networkx graph instances in the return and convert
    automatically. Detection with a module-name check is cheapest.

Regression tests::

    def test_bfs_tree_returns_fnx_graph():
        g = fnx.Graph(); g.add_edges_from([(0,1),(1,2)])
        assert isinstance(fnx.bfs_tree(g, 0), fnx.DiGraph)

    def test_random_tournament_returns_fnx_graph():
        assert isinstance(fnx.random_tournament(5, seed=42), fnx.DiGraph)

    def test_union_rename_returns_fnx_graph():
        g = fnx.Graph(); g.add_edge(1, 2)
        h = fnx.Graph(); h.add_edge(3, 4)
        assert isinstance(fnx.union(g, h, rename=('a-', 'b-')), fnx.Graph)

Scope note: this audit only checked the 3 graph-returning delegated
exports. The other delegated functions return dicts / lists / scalars
/ generators and are not affected. ``random_kernel_graph`` already
wraps correctly.

Closes: franken_networkx-7symx (subsumed by this broader scope).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:50:57.119473787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:50:57.119473787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-3ez8","title":"Port random generator create_using parity off NetworkX fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:17:08.410486815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:27:28.195804408Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T23:27:28.193062367Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-3f6j","title":"Port random_geometric_graph Minkowski p + geometric_edges list return","description":"Two parity gaps in the geometric generators: (1) random_geometric_graph declares a Minkowski p parameter but the implementation is hard-coded to Euclidean L2, so random_geometric_graph(n, r, p=1) returns wrong edge counts vs NetworkX; (2) fnx.geometric_edges has the wrong return contract — it mutates the graph and returns it, while nx.geometric_edges returns a list of edges, validates that every node has pos_name attribute, honors p and pos_name kwargs. Fix random_geometric_graph to use sum(|a-b|**p)<=radius**p per node pair. Rewrite geometric_edges to match NetworkX: list return, pos validation, p + pos_name support. Add parity tests covering p=1,2,3,inf, missing pos error, and custom pos_name.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"claude-fnx-port","created_at":"2026-04-20T21:53:13.140672172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T23:04:17.020522756Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T23:04:17.017139418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-3gcw","title":"[F2] Auto-discovery for backend dispatch","description":"**Track:** F. **Priority:** P1. **Type:** feature.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:26.167651233Z","updated_at":"2026-04-13T19:00:08.899872381Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T19:00:08.899522018Z","close_reason":"Added auto-discovery: discover_standard_backends(), DiscoveredBackend, DiscoveryReport, BackendRegistry::with_discovered_backends(). 6 new tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -215,6 +216,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_networkx-7n2w","title":"NX-REPLACE random_unlabeled_rooted_forest exact native implementation","description":"## Scope
`random_unlabeled_rooted_forest` now has exact NetworkX parity again, but it still delegates to NetworkX because the current local approximation was incorrect and the true Wilf-style combinatorial sampler is nontrivial.

## Goal
Implement the exact rooted-forest sampler natively while preserving:
- `q` semantics
- `number_of_forests` batching
- `graph["roots"]` contract
- seeded parity expectations / invariants

## Files
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py
- tests/python/test_generator_delegations_parity.py
- optionally crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs and crates/fnx-python/src/generators.rs if moved into Rust","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:30:37.901214041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T14:52:19.393873661Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T14:52:19.393508791Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-7o93","title":"port: katz_centrality_numpy convergence parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:52:39.364494290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:55:55.389482419Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:55:55.387602347Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-7plg","title":"parity: to_directed live views should support has_predecessor and has_successor","description":"The top-level to_directed() live views currently lack has_predecessor() and has_successor(), while NetworkX exposes both on the resulting directed views. On a simple converted graph, NetworkX reports True for both predecessor/successor checks on the mirrored edge, while FrankenNetworkX exposes no such methods. Match the upstream directed-query API.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:14:28.220144199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:23:40.505587343Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:23:40.503742130Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-7symx","title":"fnx.union(G, H, rename=...) returns a networkx Graph instead of a franken_networkx graph","description":"When ``rename`` is non-empty, ``fnx.union`` delegates to
``nx.union`` via the ``_union_with_rename_via_parity`` helper (see
python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:3916, landed in 057a442; this
was pre-existing shape too, visible in 057a442^).

The helper converts the inputs to nx graphs with
``_networkx_graph_for_parity``, calls ``nx.union``, and returns the
raw nx result — **no round-trip back to the fnx Python surface**.

Reproducer::

    import franken_networkx as fnx
    g = fnx.Graph(); g.add_edge(1, 2)
    h = fnx.Graph(); h.add_edge(3, 4)
    r = fnx.union(g, h, rename=('A-', 'B-'))
    # Expected: isinstance(r, fnx.Graph) is True
    # Actual  : isinstance(r, fnx.Graph) is False  (returns nx.Graph)
    # type(r).__module__ == 'networkx.classes.graph'

This breaks downstream fnx API expectations: subsequent fnx
algorithms dispatch differently on nx vs fnx graph types, and
pickling, backend caching, and PlanarEmbedding round-tripping all
assume the fnx layout.

Fix: wrap the nx return in ``_from_nx_graph`` (readwrite module)
and pick ``create_using`` based on the inputs' fnx class. e.g.::

    from franken_networkx.readwrite import _from_nx_graph
    return _from_nx_graph(
        _nx_mod.union(
            _networkx_graph_for_parity(G),
            _networkx_graph_for_parity(H),
            rename=rename,
        ),
        create_using=type(G)() if isinstance(G, (fnx.Graph, fnx.DiGraph,
                                                 fnx.MultiGraph, fnx.MultiDiGraph)) else None,
    )

Regression test: add to test_graph_operators or similar::

    def test_union_with_rename_returns_fnx_graph():
        g = fnx.Graph(); g.add_edge(1, 2)
        h = fnx.Graph(); h.add_edge(3, 4)
        r = fnx.union(g, h, rename=('a-', 'b-'))
        assert isinstance(r, fnx.Graph)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:49:41.480498033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:49:41.480498033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-7u74","title":"[E5] Wire G1→G8 CI gate topology into conformance CI workflow","description":"**Track:** E. **Priority:** P0. **Type:** feature.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:21.753437351Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:13.913282433Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:13.913079569Z","close_reason":"duplicate from dry-run double-create","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-7uk","title":"Port from_prufer_sequence and to_prufer_sequence from Python to Rust","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T03:55:41.845554301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T03:55:41.960225941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T03:55:41.959963114Z","close_reason":"Implemented both Prüfer sequence functions in Rust with PyO3 bindings. Round-trip verified, exact match with NetworkX output.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-7vbs","title":"parity: rescale_layout should preserve integer-array dtype error contract","description":"rescale_layout currently fails too early on integer coordinate arrays. On input np.array([[0, 2], [2, 0]]), FrankenNetworkX raises the same ambiguous-truthiness ValueError as soon as the wrapper sees the array, while upstream NetworkX reaches the numeric operation and raises a dtype-sensitive UFuncTypeError about casting subtract output into int64. Preserve NetworkX's integer-array failure contract for the public rescale_layout helper.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:24:59.460757183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:25:22.006384443Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:25:05.307903962Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"franken_networkx-7vbs","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fixed in latest commit (test gate). Behaviour aligned by ede0bf0 'rescale_layout accepts NumPy array inputs': integer-array input now flows into the in-place subtract and raises the same _UFuncOutputCastingError as nx, not the early ambiguous-truthiness ValueError. Gate covers both fnx and nx behavior on the canonical np.array([[0, 2], [2, 0]]) input.","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:25:22Z"}]}
@@ -276,6 +278,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_networkx-9vei","title":"[D2k] Thread RuntimePolicy through spanning-tree iterators","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T03:40:21.892141370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T03:43:07.448979228Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T03:43:07.446096217Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-9vr6","title":"parity: single_source_all_shortest_paths should return node->path-list pairs","description":"single_source_all_shortest_paths currently yields bare paths instead of (node, [[paths...]]) pairs, drops equal shortest paths, and misses NetworkX's method-parity surface. On a weighted diamond graph from a->e, FrankenNetworkX returns [['a'], ['a','b'], ['a','b','d'], ['a','b','e'], ['a','c']] while NetworkX returns [('a', [['a']]), ('b', [['a','b']]), ('c', [['a','c']]), ('d', [['a','b','d'], ['a','c','d']]), ('e', [['a','b','e'], ['a','b','d','e'], ['a','c','d','e']])]. Match the generator contract, include the source/self entry, preserve all equal shortest simple paths, and cover weighted/unweighted parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:28:21.611534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:18:12.283213881Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:18:12.281146289Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in commit b901108","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-9wbo","title":"port: erdos_renyi_graph seed reproducibility parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:16:54.875357693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:19:07.398550681Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:19:07.396539765Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-9x7r0","title":"MultiGraph.copy() crashes when an edge attribute is named 'key'","description":"``_copy_preserving_insertion_order`` (landed in ec93156) iterates
edges and passes ``key`` as a keyword argument *and* splats the
edge attribute dict via ``**deepcopy(attrs)``:

    for u, v, key, attrs in self.edges(keys=True, data=True):
        result.add_edge(u, v, key=key, **deepcopy(attrs))

When an edge has an attribute literally named ``key`` (which NetworkX
permits — nothing prevents ``G['a']['b'][0]['key'] = 'value'``), this
raises:

    TypeError: MultiGraph.add_edge() got multiple values for keyword
    argument 'key'

Reproducer:

    import franken_networkx as fnx
    g = fnx.MultiGraph()
    g.add_edge('a', 'b', weight=10)
    g['a']['b'][0]['key'] = 'stored_value'
    g.copy()  # TypeError

Upstream nx.MultiGraph.copy avoids this by using ``add_edges_from``
with 4-tuples ``(u, v, key, datadict)``; the datadict goes in
positionally so ``key`` is just another dict entry and doesn't clash
with the positional key arg.

Fix: switch ``_copy_preserving_insertion_order`` to the same
add_edges_from / 4-tuple pattern. Same pattern also needs auditing
in ``_to_undirected_with_view`` (landed in 7cafa35), which has the
same ``result.add_edge(u, v, key=key, **deepcopy(attrs))`` shape
for MultiGraph undirected.

Add a regression test: copy a MultiGraph whose edge has an attribute
named ``key``; assert the copy round-trips the attribute dict intact.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:45:26.398444148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:45:26.398444148Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-9yad","title":"[A2] .gitignore audit and re-baseline","description":"**Track:** A. **Priority:** P0. **Type:** task.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:26.167651233Z","updated_at":"2026-04-13T23:15:44.845670273Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T23:15:44.845399540Z","close_reason":"Expand .gitignore with root-level agent debris patterns","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-a0n","title":"Expose missing Python bindings for existing Rust algorithms","description":"Many Rust algorithms in fnx-algorithms have directed variants and additional functions that aren't exposed through the Python binding layer. Need to wrap ~30+ functions in fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs and expose them in python/franken_networkx/__init__.py. Includes: all-pairs shortest path variants, directed BFS/DFS variants, directed centrality variants, graph metrics (boundary expansion, conductance), and average_node_connectivity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:05:04.396834021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:19:18.355427237Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T03:19:18.354889127Z","close_reason":"Added 12 new Python bindings: volume, boundary_expansion, conductance, edge_expansion, node_expansion, mixing_expansion, non_edges, average_node_connectivity, is_k_edge_connected, all_pairs_dijkstra, number_of_spanning_arborescences, global_node_connectivity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-a0qm","title":"[E7] Structure-aware fuzzers for algorithm code paths","description":"**Track:** E. **Priority:** P1. **Type:** feature.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:26.167651233Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T19:22:35.848339151Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T19:22:35.845438147Z","close_reason":"Implemented 7 structure-aware algorithm fuzzers using arbitrary::Arbitrary: fuzz_shortest_path (Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, single/multi-source), fuzz_centrality (PageRank, betweenness, closeness, HITS, Katz, eigenvector, harmonic), fuzz_connectivity (components, articulation points, bridges, node/edge connectivity), fuzz_matching (maximal, max/min weight), fuzz_flow (Edmonds-Karp max flow, min cut), fuzz_spanning_tree (MST Kruskal/Prim, is_tree/is_forest), fuzz_clustering (coefficients, k-core, k-truss, bipartite, coloring). Added to PR CI smoke (30s each) and nightly soak (1h each). Committed seed corpora.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-a0qm","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-aeaw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:43.632899723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
